title: Spermacoce xanthophylla
intro: Spermacoce xanthophylla, also known as yellow centaury, is a flowering plant in the Rubiaceae family, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various health conditions such as fever, colds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Spermacoce xanthophylla include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Spermacoce xanthophylla is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often employed in traditional African medicine and also used in some modern herbal remedies.
taxonomy: Spermacoce xanthophylla is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the genus Spermacoce, which consists of several species of plants commonly known as bugleheads or false bugleheads. Spermacoce xanthophylla is native to Australia, where it can be found growing in a variety of habitats. The plant is often classified as a shrub or a vine, with a sprawling habit and small white or yellow flowers. Within the Rubiaceae family, Spermacoce xanthophylla is classified in the tribe Spermacoceae.
uses: Spermacoce xanthophylla, also known as Yellow Mitchell's joyweed, is a plant species that has been reported to have various medicinal uses. Traditionally, it has been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which could be beneficial in treating infections. Its medicinal uses are primarily based on traditional knowledge and have not been extensively scientifically studied.

parts: Spermacoce xanthophylla, also known as yellow mint, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The medicinal parts of this plant are primarily the leaves, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
